name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمَ لِتَرْكَبُوا۟ مِنْهَا وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ (٧٩)
```

```english
It is Allah who created the cattle for you that you may ride some of them, and some of them you eat; (79)
```

```arabic
وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَـٰفِعُ وَلِتَبْلُغُوا۟ عَلَيْهَا حَاجَةً فِى صُدُورِكُمْ وَعَلَيْهَا وَعَلَى ٱلْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ (٨٠)
```

```english
and there are [numerous] uses in them for you, and that over them you may satisfy any need that is in your breasts, and you are carried on them and on ships. (80)
```

```arabic
وَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ فَأَىَّ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ تُنكِرُونَ (٨١)
```

```english
He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny? (81)
```

```arabic
أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَانُوٓا۟ أَكْثَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ قُوَّةً وَءَاثَارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (٨٢)
```

```english
Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more numerous than them and were greater [than them] in power and with respect to the effects [they left] in the land. But what they used to earn did not avail them. (82)
```

```arabic
فَلَمَّا جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَرِحُوا۟ بِمَا عِندَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٨٣)
```

```english
When their apostles brought them manifest proofs, they boasted about the knowledge they possessed, but they were besieged by what they used to deride. (83)
```

```arabic
فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا۟ بَأْسَنَا قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَحْدَهُۥ وَكَفَرْنَا بِمَا كُنَّا بِهِۦ مُشْرِكِينَ (٨٤)
```

```english
Then, when they sighted Our punishment, they said, ‘We believe in Allah alone, and disavow what we used to take as His partners.’ (84)
```

```arabic
فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنفَعُهُمْ إِيمَـٰنُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا۟ بَأْسَنَا ۖ سُنَّتَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى قَدْ خَلَتْ فِى عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (٨٥)
```

```english
But their faith was of no benefit to them when they sighted Our punishment—Allah’s precedent, which has passed among His servants, and it is thence that the faithless will be losers. (85)
```

## (Commentary)

This is a return, after a previous return, to the mention of some signs of monotheism (التوحيد) — *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts* — and a redirection of the people toward taking heed from the state of the past, perished nations and the ongoing custom (سنة) of Allah regarding them. This custom involved sending His messengers to them and then judging between the messengers and their people, leading to the perdition of the disbelievers among them; at this point, the Surah concludes.

His saying, the Exalted: **اللَّهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعَامَ لِتَرْكَبُوا مِنْهَا وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ** "It is Allah who made the cattle for you then you may ride some of them, and some of them you eat" (40:79). The Glorified mentions the livestock (الأنعام) — referring to camels, cows, and sheep — among the things from which man derives benefit in his life and through which he manages his affairs. It has also been said that the intended meaning here is specifically camels.

Thus, His saying: "made the cattle for you" (جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعَامَ), the "making" (الجعل) here refers to creation (الخلق) or subjugation (التسخير). The "lam" in "that you may ride" (لِتَرْكَبُوا) denotes purpose, and "from" (من) denotes partitivity (التبعيض). The meaning is: He created for your sake, or subjugated for you, the livestock, and the purpose of this making is that you ride some of them, such as certain camels, and from some of them, such as other camels, cows, and sheep, you eat.

His saying, the Exalted: **وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا مَنَافِعُ** "And there are [other] profits in them for you" (40:80), such as your benefiting from their milk, wool, fur, hair, hides, and other such things. His saying: **وَلِتَبْلُغُوا عَلَيْهَا حَاجَةً فِي صُدُورِكُمْ** "and that through them you may satisfy a need in your breasts" (40:80) means: and among the purposes of making them is that you may reach, while being upon them by way of riding, a need in your breasts, which is the movement from one place to another for various objectives.

His saying: **وَعَلَيْهَا وَعَلَى الْفُلْكِ تُحْمَلُونَ** "and you are carried on them and on the ships" (40:80) is a metaphorical expression for traversing land and sea by means of livestock and ships.

His saying, the Exalted: **وَيُرِيكُمْ آيَاتِهِ فَأَيَّ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُنْكِرُونَ** "He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?" (40:81). The meaning of His showing His signs has already been discussed in the commentary on the beginning of the Surah. It is as if the sentence—meaning His saying: "He shows you His signs" (وَيُرِيكُمْ آيَاتِهِ)—is not intended for its own sake, which would necessitate repetition, but rather as a preamble and a preparation for the rebuke contained in His saying: "So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?" (فَأَيَّ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُنْكِرُونَ). That is: which of these signs that Allah shows you clearly and demonstrably do you deny with a denial that paves the way for you to turn away from His monotheism?

His saying, the Exalted: **أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا** "Have they not travelled through the land and seen..." (40:82) until the end of the verse, is a rebuke to them and a redirection of their sight toward what has occurred of the custom (سنة) of judgment and decree regarding the past nations. A similar verse appeared at the beginning of the Surah, where the purpose was to clarify to them that Allah seized each of them for their sins because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs (البينات) but they disbelieved in them; hence, that verse concluded with His saying: "so Allah seized them for their sins" (فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ). The purpose here, however, is to clarify to them that what they earned did not avail them, nor did the knowledge they possessed and exulted in benefit them in averting the punishment of Allah, nor did their repentance and regret for what they had done.

The verse begins with the "fa" of derivation (فاء التفريع), as it says: "Have they not travelled..." (أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا), along with a transition (التفات) from direct address to the third person. It is as if the speech is derived from His saying: "So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?" (فَأَيَّ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُنْكِرُونَ). Thus, after censuring them and denouncing their denial of His signs, He turned away from them toward the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله, indicating their fall from the status of being addressed, and said: If His signs are manifest and clear, accepting no denial—and among them are the signs speaking through the ruins of the predecessors—and they have indeed travelled through the land and witnessed them, then why did they not look into them so it would become clear to them that the predecessors, despite being greater than these people in quantity and quality, were not benefited by the knowledge and power they exulted in?

His saying, the Exalted: **فَلَمَّا جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَرِحُوا بِمَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ** "When their apostles brought them manifest proofs, they exulted in the knowledge they possessed..." (40:83). The plural pronouns in the verse—of which there are seven—refer to those who were before them. The "knowledge they possessed" (بِمَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ) refers to what had settled in their hearts and occupied their souls regarding the adornment of the worldly life and the various methods of management to attain it and reach its pleasures. Allah, the Glorified, has counted that as "knowledge" for them and restricted their knowledge to it, as He says: **يَعْلَمُونَ ظَاهِراً مِنَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَهُمْ عَنِ الْآخِرَةِ هُمْ غَافِلُونَ** "They know just an outward aspect of the life of the world, but they are oblivious of the Hereafter" (30:7), and He says: **فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْ مَنْ تَوَلَّى عَنْ ذِكْرِنَا وَلَمْ يُرِدْ إِلَّا الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا ذَلِكَ مَبْلَغُهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ** "So avoid those who turn away from Our remembrance and desire nothing but the life of the world. That is the ultimate reach of their knowledge" (53:29-30).

The meaning of their "exulting in the knowledge they possessed" is their intense admiration for the expertise and outward knowledge they had acquired, and their attraction to it, which led to their turning away from the true gnosis (المعارف الحقيقية) brought by their messengers, as well as their belittling and mocking of it. Therefore, He followed their exulting in the knowledge they possessed with His saying: **وَحَاقَ بِهِمْ مَا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ** "and they were besieged by what they used to deride" (40:83).

Regarding the meaning of His saying: "they exulted in the knowledge they possessed" (فَرِحُوا بِمَا عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ), there are other opinions:

Among them: that the "knowledge they possessed" refers to their corrupt beliefs and false opinions, and calling them "knowledge" is by way of sarcasm (التهكم); they used to exult in them and consequently despise the knowledge of the messengers. You are well aware that this is a characterization without evidence.

Among them: that the "knowledge" refers to the sciences of the Greek philosophers and the materialists (الدهريين); when they heard of revelation and the knowledge of prophecy, they belittled the knowledge of the prophets and boasted of what they possessed. This is like the previous one, in addition to the fact that it does not apply to any of the nations whose stories the Quran narrates, such as the people of Nuh, 'Ad, Thamud, the people of Ibrahim, the people of Lut, the people of Shu'ayb, and others.

Among them: that the original meaning is: "When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they did *not* exult in the knowledge that came to them," so "they exulted in the ignorance they possessed" was put in its place, and then "ignorance" was replaced with "knowledge" sarcastically, resulting in: "they exulted in the knowledge they possessed." This view—besides its artificiality (التكلف) and distance from common understanding—is subject to the same objections as the first.

Among them: that the pronoun in "they exulted" (فَرِحُوا) refers to the disbelievers, while the pronoun in "they possessed" (عِنْدَهُمْ) refers to the messengers. The meaning would be: the disbelievers exulted in the knowledge the messengers possessed with the exultation of laughter and mockery. The flaw here is that it necessitates a discrepancy in the consistent pronouns, in addition to the fact that laughter and mockery are not called "exultation" (فرح) without a contextual indicator.

Among them: that both pronouns in "they exulted in what they possessed" (فَرِحُوا بِمَا عِنْدَهُمْ) refer to the messengers. The meaning would be: when the messengers came to them and witnessed their ignorance, their persistence in disbelief and denial, and knew the consequence of their affair, they exulted in the true knowledge (العلم الحق) they possessed and thanked Allah for that.

The flaw here is that the context of the verses is the strongest witness that they were set forth to describe the state of the disbelievers after the messengers brought them clear proofs, and how it resulted in the descent of punishment, and how faith did not benefit them after witnessing the might (البأس). What connection does this have to the messengers exulting in their true knowledge? Furthermore, this also necessitates a discrepancy in the pronouns.

His saying, the Exalted: **فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنَا قَالُوا آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ وَحْدَهُ وَكَفَرْنَا بِمَا كُنَّا بِهِ مُشْرِكِينَ** "Then, when they sighted Our punishment, they said, ‘We believe in Allah alone, and we disbelieve in what we used to ascribe as partners to Him’" (40:84). **The might** (البأس) is the severity of the punishment, and the rest is clear.

His saying, the Exalted: **فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنْفَعُهُمْ إِيمَانُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنَا** "But their faith did not benefit them when they sighted Our punishment..." (40:85). This is because faith at that moment is not based on free choice (الاختيار). His saying: **سُنَّتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي قَدْ خَلَتْ فِي عِبَادِهِ** "[This is] Allah’s precedent which has passed among His servants" (40:85) means: Allah has established it as an ongoing custom (سنة) among His servants that no repentance is accepted after the sighting of the punishment. **وَخَسِرَ هُنَالِكَ الْكَافِرُونَ** "and there the faithless were the losers" (40:85).
